page: Vultr vbm-8c-128gb-amd is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal NVMe family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 4345P processor built on the x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is configured with 123.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, which provides 7.71 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a 3890 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU hardware is present in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests and single-threaded compression and decompression benchmarks scoring in the top 10%. Passmark memory benchmarks also rank in the top tier. Multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded decompression, and Redis SET operations perform within the average range. A notable hardware tradeoff is the large-block memory write bandwidth, which scores in the weak bottom 25% tier, while read bandwidth remains average. For language model inference, the server delivers strong prompt processing and top-tier text generation speeds on smaller models.

This bare metal configuration provides high resource density and dedicated hardware access, offering qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require sustained CPU performance and fast local storage without virtualization overhead. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores, high-speed DDR5 memory, and a large NVMe SSD makes this server well-suited for database hosting, static web serving, memory-intensive applications, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks.
description: Vultr vbm-8c-128gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring a dedicated 16-core AMD EPYC 4345P processor running at 3.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 123.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory and a 3890 GB local NVMe SSD, with no GPU included. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside strong performance in single-threaded compression and small-model LLM text generation. However, large-block memory write bandwidth represents a relative performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25%. This server provides high resource density and dedicated hardware access, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that demand sustained processing power and fast local storage. It is highly suitable for database hosting, static web serving, and memory-intensive applications.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining high-speed DDR5 memory and large NVMe storage for demanding database and compute workloads.
